item.title: Emerging Stocks Slip From Highs as Tech Selloff, Oil Surge Weigh
item.description: Emerging stocks fell on Tuesday, retreating from record highs, as a broad selloff in tech shares and higher oil prices dragged on sentiment.

item.title: Avia Bonds Fall Into Distress as Iran War Roils Travel Industry
item.description: Avia Solutions Group’s bonds have fallen into distressed territory as turmoil in the travel industry caused by the Middle East war dampens demand for the aviation company’s debt.

item.title: VC Firm Hires First Chief AI Officer, Seeking Smarter Use of the Technology
item.description: Eclipse wants to help its portfolio companies get smarter with AI

item.title: Bank of Japan Delivers Hawkish Hold But Yen Remains on Brink
item.description: BOJ Governor Ueda treads carefully as the yen hovers near a level that’s prompted past intervention.

item.title: Global Goods Trade Set to Slow This Year to Half 2025’s Pace
item.description: The oil price shock and protectionism will act as headwinds for cross-border commerce

item.title: Bank of Italy Urges Meloni Government to Keep Eye on Spending
item.description: Italy needs to watch its spending if it wants to stick to European Union fiscal rules despite a worsening economic growth scenario, Bank of Italy official Andrea Brandolini said.

item.title: Barrick Picks New York for Gold Spinoff Later This Year
item.description: Barrick Mining Corp. said it plans to list its North American operations in New York and reaffirmed that it’s on track to complete the initial public offering by the end of this year.

item.title: Danish Jobs Drop, Snapping Growth Streak as Novo Cuts Hit Data
item.description: Denmark’s employed workforce shrank for the first time since mid-2024, as layoffs at Novo Nordisk A/S began to show up in official data.

item.title: US Treasuries Trade Rangebound as Fed Officials Prepare to Meet
item.description: Treasuries fluctuated in a range as Federal Reserve officials prepared to meet in what’s expected to be Jerome Powell’s final meeting as head of the central bank.

item.title: Tech’s ‘New Normal’ Trade Pair: Long Chip Stock, Short Software
item.description: In a choppy year for tech investors, one trade has stood out as a success: buy chip stocks, sell software shares. And the divide between winners and losers is getting bigger as 2026 moves along.

item.title: Chinese Billionaire Overhauls AI Startup After Warning on Manus
item.description: The fallout from Meta Platforms Inc.’s controversial acquisition of Manus is prompting one tech founder to erect strict walls between his Chinese and US businesses, calling it a regrettable but necessary template for navigating geopolitical tensions.

item.title: BYD Quarterly Profit Drops to Lowest in Years Amid Price War
item.description: BYD Co.’s quarterly profit tumbled to its lowest level in more than three years as the the world’s largest maker of electric vehicles offered more discounts on its cars to fend off mounting competition.

item.title: GoTo Posts First-Ever Net Income After Cost Cuts Bear Fruit
item.description: GoTo Group reported its first-ever net income, a major milestone in the Indonesian ride-hailing and food delivery company’s turnaround effort as it seeks to gain fresh momentum in its long-standing rivalry with Grab Holdings Ltd.

item.title: JPMorgan Says Firms Avoid Raising Forecasts Due to War Concerns
item.description: Doubts triggered by the Middle East conflict are feeding into US earnings, with some companies refraining from increasing forecasts despite a strong start to the year, according to strategists at JPMorgan Chase & Co.

item.title: Funds Pitching SpaceX Rush to Lure Retail Investors Before IPO
item.description: Demand drove one fund to a 3,000% premium to net asset value

item.title: UK Bond Selloff Sends 10-Year Yield Back to 5% Ahead of BOE Meet
item.description: The UK’s government bonds are sliding on pressure from surging oil prices and political risks, sending the 10-year yield back above 5% for the first time in a month.

item.title: Spotify Grows Subscriber Count Even After Recent Price Hikes
item.description: Spotify Technology SA, the music streaming leader, added slightly more users than analysts’ expected in the first quarter, showing it can still pull in new listeners even after it raised prices in its biggest market.

item.title: Most Prediction Market Traders Are Losing Money While Bots Rack Up Gains
item.description: Data from millions of accounts shows that trading and profits are dominated by a tiny slice of highly active accounts.

item.title: BlackRock Targets the Idle Cash Piling Up on Crypto Exchanges
item.description: BlackRock Inc. is bringing its roughly $2.5 billion money market fund to cryptocurrency exchange operator OKX, with Standard Chartered Plc holding the underlying assets — the latest sign that Wall Street infrastructure and digital-asset markets are converging.

item.title: Defense Space Startup True Anomaly Raises $650 Million
item.description: True Anomaly has raised $650 million in a fresh funding round, bringing the defense aerospace startup’s valuation to $2.2 billion as it looks to hire more employees and scale up its satellite business.

item.title: Oil Shock Starts to Seep Into Earnings and Stock Prices
item.description: More bitter surprises may be in store in this week’s flood of results.

item.title: US Investor Said to Pay 70% Premium for Benfica Minority Stake
item.description: US investor Entrepreneur Equity Partners has agreed to buy a minority stake in Portuguese football club Benfica at a steep premium, according to a person familiar with the matter.

item.title: Atlas Copco’s Chipmaker-Exposed Unit Shines as Orders Disappoint
item.description: Atlas Copco AB’s chipmaking-related business grew twice as strong as analysts expected, a bright spot for the company as other units disappointed.

item.title: Tullow Shares Soar as West African Oil Brings Record Price
item.description: Tullow Oil Plc’s shares surged more than 9% after tensions in the Middle East drove its West African crude to a record price.

item.title: Ping An Profit Falls as Market Decline Hurts Investment Returns
item.description: Ping An Insurance (Group) Co. said profit fell 7.4% in the first quarter, as declines in China’s stock market dented investment returns.

item.title: Maruti Profit Misses on Rising Costs, Supply Constraints
item.description: Maruti Suzuki India Ltd. reported fourth-quarter profit that missed analysts’ estimates, with higher costs and supply constraints weighing on margins.

item.title: Barclays Reports £66 Billion Exposure to Non-Bank Lending
item.description: Barclays Plc laid out its £66 billion ($89.2 billion) of structured financing exposure to non-banks, becoming the latest lender to make disclosures in an area that’s unnerved investors lately.

item.title: Europe Spent €10 Billion on Iran Energy Shock Aid, Bruegel Says
item.description: European Union states have committed over €10 billion ($11.7 billion) to shield consumers and businesses as the Iran war drives up energy prices, according to the Bruegel think tank.

item.title: Hedge Fund Short Covering Fuels Demand in $5 Billion CATL Deal
item.description: Hedge funds bought the bulk of the shares in Contemporary Amperex Technology Co. Ltd.’s $5 billion placement, with at least some of the demand driven by traders seeking to cover short positions on the battery maker’s Hong Kong-listed shares, according to people with knowledge of the matter.

item.title: Norway, Sweden Are Seen Hiking Rates by Early 2027 in Survey
item.description: Economists surveyed by Bloomberg now expect both Norges Bank and the Riksbank to tighten monetary policy within the next 12 months.
